findings.summary: Logging:LogLevel in appsettings.json is dead configuration — Serilog never calls ReadFrom.Configuration, so it has zero effect.
findings.detail: Editing appsettings.json's Logging:LogLevel would silently do nothing since Log.Logger is built entirely in code. Same in EventLedger.AccountService and both appsettings.Development.json files.

disposition.reasoning: Removed the misleading Logging:LogLevel section from all four appsettings*.json files rather than adding an undocumented ReadFrom.Configuration call — standards/logging-dotnet.md's snippet doesn't call for config-driven levels, so removing the dead section matches the documented standard instead of extending it.

findings.summary: Claimed: ASP.NET Core's built-in "Request starting"/"Request finished" hosting-diagnostics logs run outside this middleware's LogContext scope and won't carry TraceId.
findings.detail: The middleware-scope reasoning is correct in isolation (hosting diagnostics logs do run outside app.Use() middleware), but the conclusion is refuted by direct empirical test: captured log output shows the "Request starting" line (SourceContext: Microsoft.AspNetCore.Hosting.Diagnostics) DOES carry a real TraceId (e.g. "cd32c3a6fc744b07a934bccc3fc4cde7"). Serilog 3.x's LogEvent has native TraceId/SpanId fields sourced directly from Activity.Current, independent of LogContext.PushProperty — this covers the hosting-diagnostics logs regardless of custom middleware scope.

disposition.reasoning: Verified false positive — re-ran a targeted diagnostic test against the actual running application and confirmed the hosting-diagnostics "Request starting" line already carries a correct, non-null TraceId via Serilog's built-in Activity-aware enrichment, not via this middleware. No code change needed; the described gap doesn't exist in practice.

findings.summary: No try/catch/finally + Log.CloseAndFlush() around startup — a startup crash wouldn't be captured in the JSON log stream and buffered sink state wouldn't flush on shutdown.

disposition.reasoning: Wrapped builder/app construction and app.Run() in try/catch(Exception)/finally with Log.Fatal + Log.CloseAndFlush(), matching the standard Serilog+ASP.NET Core bootstrap idiom, in both services.

findings.summary: Application/ and Domain/ folders from the documented repository tree aren't present in the commit — git doesn't track empty directories.

disposition.reasoning: Deliberate, pre-existing decision (no .gitkeep placeholders, per this project's anti-cruft convention) — self-resolves the moment issue #2 adds the first file into either folder. Not a real gap.

findings.summary: launchBrowser: true on API-only services with no browsable UI opens a browser tab to a bare JSON endpoint on every dotnet run.

disposition.reasoning: Set launchBrowser to false across all profiles in both services' launchSettings.json.

findings.summary: Logging test hits GET / (unmapped, 404) instead of the now-existing GET /health — stale route choice never updated after Phase 4 added the real endpoint.

disposition.reasoning: Changed both logging tests to hit /health instead of /.

findings.summary: Assert.NotNull(response) is always true for a completed HTTP call (failures throw, not return null) — the assertion adds no real signal beyond "the call didn't throw".

disposition.reasoning: Changed both boot tests to assert response.IsSuccessStatusCode with a descriptive failure message.

findings.summary: JSON log line validated via substring Contains/regex rather than parsing as JSON and asserting individual properties.

disposition.reasoning: Rewrote both logging tests to JsonDocument.Parse the matched line and assert on RootElement.GetProperty("TraceId") and Properties.GetProperty("ServiceName") directly, applied together with the F6 route fix.

findings.summary: GET /health's DB-connectivity contract is temporarily unmet by this scaffold, and that gap is tracked only in docs/plans/, not in architecture/ itself — architecture-advisor only reads architecture/, so it would treat the fuller contract as already true.

disposition.reasoning: Added a sequencing note directly in architecture/observability.md's Health checks section, explaining the temporary gap and pointing to issue #5, so architecture-advisor (which only reads architecture/) doesn't treat the full contract as already implemented.
